message.content: I want to refactor the authentication module to use JWT tokens instead of session cookies.

content.text: Good idea. JWT tokens are more suitable for your API-first architecture. Let me look at the current auth module.

message.content: Yes, and I prefer using the jose library over jsonwebtoken — it has better TypeScript support and is more actively maintained.

content.text: Noted — jose over jsonwebtoken for JWT handling. Let me update the implementation.

input.command: soulsys add-memory "User prefers jose library over jsonwebtoken for JWT — better TypeScript support" --importance 6

content.text: I've saved that preference. Now let me implement the JWT middleware using jose.

message.content: We also discovered that the refresh token rotation wasn't working because the old token wasn't being invalidated in the database. That was a tricky bug.

content.text: That's a significant finding. The refresh token rotation bug was caused by the invalidation query not matching on the token family ID. I've fixed it by adding the family constraint to the DELETE query.
